attributes.action: Allow Trans People to Use Toilets Aligned with Their Gender Identity
attributes.additional_details:
Forcing trans people to use facilities matching their sex at birth causes severe harm: 49% have faced abuse in public toilets, 30% restrict eating/drinking to avoid them, and 42% say fear stops them from working or leaving home. Trans people face 4x more hate generally, and pose no credible risk to other, the opposite is a harmful myth.
Under the Equality Act, trans people must be treated by their gender identity. We demand clear rules for inclusive toilet access based on evidence not prejudice.
attributes.background: We call on all relevant authorities and service providers to formalise the right for trans people to use public toilet facilities that match their gender identity. This is a basic matter of dignity, safety, and equality, and is supported by official guidance, evidence, and human rights principles.

rejection.details:
There is a petition open calling for the disapproval of the draft Equality Act 2010 guidance, laid on 21 May 2026 which you may wish to sign: 

'Disapprove the draft Equality Act 2010 guidance laid on 21 May 2026'
https://petition.parliament.uk/petitions/770527
